2604.20300.title: FSFM: A Biologically-Inspired Framework for Selective Forgetting of Agent Memory
2604.20300.paper_summary: For LLM agents, memory management critically impacts efficiency, quality, and security. While much research focuses on retention, selective forgetting--inspired by human cognitive processes (hippocampal indexing/consolidation theory and Ebbinghaus forgetting curve)--remains underexplored. We argue that in resource-constrained environments, a well-designed forgetting mechanism is as crucial as remembering, delivering benefits across three dimensions: (1) efficiency via intelligent memory pruning, (2) quality by dynamically updating outdated preferences and context, and (3) security through active forgetting of malicious inputs, sensitive data, and privacy-compromising content. Our framework establishes a taxonomy of forgetting mechanisms: passive decay-based, active deletion-based, safety-triggered, and adaptive reinforcement-based. Building on advances in LLM agent architectures and vector databases, we present detailed specifications, implementation strategies, and empirical validation from controlled experiments. Results show significant improvements: access efficiency (+8.49%), content quality (+29.2% signal-to-noise ratio), and security performance (100% elimination of security risks). Our work bridges cognitive neuroscience and AI systems, offering practical solutions for real-world deployment while addressing ethical and regulatory compliance. The paper concludes with challenges and future directions, establishing selective forgetting as a fundamental capability for next-generation LLM agents operating in real-world, resource-constrained scenarios. Our contributions align with AI-native memory systems and responsible AI development.
2604.20300.paper_summary_zh: 對於 LLM 代理而言，記憶管理對效率、質量和安全性有著關鍵影響。雖然許多研究集中於保留，但選擇性遺忘——受到人類認知過程（海馬體索引/鞏固理論和艾賓浩斯遺忘曲線）的啟發——仍然未被充分探討。我們主張，在資源受限的環境中，設計良好的遺忘機制與記憶一樣重要，並在三個維度上帶來好處：（1）通過智能記憶修剪提高效率，（2）通過動態更新過時的偏好和上下文提高質量，以及（3）通過主動遺忘惡意輸入、敏感數據和侵犯隱私的內容來提高安全性。我們的框架建立了一個遺忘機制的分類法：基於被動衰減、基於主動刪除、安全觸發和基於自適應增強。基於 LLM 代理架構和向量數據庫的進展，我們提出了詳細的規範、實施策略和來自控制實驗的實證驗證。結果顯示顯著改進：訪問效率提高 (+8.49%)，內容質量提高 (+29.2% 信噪比)，以及安全性能 (100% 消除安全風險)。我們的工作架起了認知神經科學與 AI 系統之間的橋樑，提供了實際解決方案以便在現實世界中部署，同時解決倫理和法規遵從問題。本文以挑戰和未來方向作結，確立選擇性遺忘作為下一代 LLM 代理在現實世界資源受限場景中運作的基本能力。我們的貢獻與 AI 原生記憶系統和負責任的 AI 發展相一致。
2604.20300.author: Yingjie Gu et.al.
2604.20300.authors: Yingjie Gu, Bo Xiong, Yijuan Guo, Chao Li, Xiaojing Zhang, Liqiang Wang, Pengcheng Ren, Qi Sun, Jingyao Ma, Shidang Shi
